PRL_Recent.title: Effective Field Theory Constraints on Primordial Black Holes from the High-Redshift Lyman-$α$ Forest
PRL_Recent.summary: Author(s): Mikhail M. Ivanov and Sokratis Trifinopoulos We present updated constraints on the abundance of primordial black hole (PBH) dark matter from the high-redshift Lyman-$α$ forest data from MIKE/HIRES experiments. Our analysis leverages an effective field theory (EFT) description of the 1D flux power spectrum, allowing us to analytically predict t… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 171402] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026

PRL_Recent.title: Observation of $\overline{\mathrm{Λ}}p→{K}^{+}{π}^{+}{π}^{−}{π}^{0}$ and $\overline{\mathrm{Λ}}p→{K}^{+}{π}^{+}{π}^{−}2{π}^{0}$
PRL_Recent.summary: Author(s): M. Ablikim et al. Rev. Lett. 136, 171904] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026

PRL_Recent.title: Topological Robustness of Anyon Tunneling at $ν=1/3$
PRL_Recent.summary: Author(s): Adithya Suresh, Ramon Guerrero-Suarez, Tanmay Maiti, Shuang Liang, Geoffrey Gardner, Claudio Chamon, and Michael Manfra In a chiral Luttinger liquid the scaling exponent for the 1/3 edge mode is insensitive to perturbations within the incompressible fractional quantum Hall effect state and establishes the bulk-boundary correspondence for topologically ordered states. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176602] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026

PRL_Recent.title: Coherent Subgap Transport in Spin-Split Josephson Junctions
PRL_Recent.summary: Author(s): David Caldevilla-Asenjo, Gorm Ole Steffensen, Sara Catalano, Alberto Hijano, Maxim Ilyn, Celia Rogero, Ramon Aguado, F. Sebastian Bergeret, and Alfredo Levy Yeyati We report the first experimental observation of subgap transport in ferromagnetic insulator–superconductor–insulator–superconductor junctions realized in $\mathrm{EuS}/\mathrm{Al}/\mathrm{AlOx}/\mathrm{Al}$ vertical stacks. Differential conductance measurements reveal multiple Andreev reflection pea… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176301] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026

PRB_Recent.title: Coexistence of moiré potential and electrostatic potential in twisted bilayer ${\mathrm{graphene}/\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ heterostructure quantum dots
PRB_Recent.summary: Author(s): Hao Sheng, Ke Lv, Mo-Han Zhang, Ya-Ning Ren, Xiao-Feng Zhou, and Lin He Twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) emerges as an ideal platform for engineering electronic states owing to its moiré superlattice and tunable electronic structure. Although the intrinsic properties of TBG have been widely studied, its electronic behavior under quantum confinement remains largely unexplo… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195403] Published Mon May 04, 2026
